evidence: In 2025 we built out our AI training offerings so all employees can develop this important future skill and contribute to our AI everywhere for everyone philosophy

innovation in products and services, artificial intelligence...transition to a platforms operating model
the development and use of artificial intelligence present risks and challenges that may materially adversely impact our business

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI-attributable spend, headcount, or capex figures appear in the excerpts; named initiative (AI training build-out) supports score 3 but not 4.

ai_narrative_concreteness: 'AI everywhere for everyone' and training build-out are named initiatives but no deployment metrics or production use-case detail are provided; score 3 reflects named program without quantified outcomes.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and names AI as a specific risk category with a mechanism ('materially adversely impact our business'), but no quantified risk scenarios or named governance/oversight bodies appear in the excerpts, capping at score 3.
Excerpts are limited; fuller filing sections (e.g., detailed risk factors, MD&A) may contain additional AI investment or risk detail not visible here.
